text: The Scaled Dot-Product Attention is defined mathematically as {{c1::\(\text{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \text{softmax}\left(\frac{QK^T}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right)V\)}}
explanation: This attention formula matches Queries (Q) against Keys (K) to create an attention score matrix. The matrix is divided by scaling factor \(\sqrt{d_k}\) to prevent dot product values from growing too large, which would push the softmax into regions with vanishing gradients.
usage:
Formula component details:
  • \(Q, K, V\): Query, Key, and Value matrices.
  • \(d_k\): Dimensionality of the key vectors.
  • \(\text{softmax}\): Normalizes attention weights to sum up to 1.
spanish: La atención de producto punto escalada se define matemáticamente como \(\text{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \text{softmax}\left(\frac{QK^T}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right)V\).

text: During auto-regressive decoding, the {{c1::KV Cache}} is used to store past key and value vectors, reducing attention computation time complexity from {{c1::\(O(N^2)\)}} to {{c1::\(O(N)\)}}.
explanation: At each decoding step, LLMs generate one token at a time. Instead of recalculating Queries, Keys, and Values for all previous tokens in the sequence, the Key and Value vectors are saved in GPU memory (cached). Only the Query of the new token needs to match against the cached Keys and Values.
usage:
Implications:
  • Saves massive compute during inference.
  • Trades compute overhead for high GPU memory consumption.
  • Optimized by architectures like Grouped-Query Attention (GQA).
spanish: Durante la decodificación autorregresiva, el KV Cache se usa para almacenar vectores clave y de valor pasados, reduciendo la complejidad del cálculo de atención de O(N^2) a O(N).

text: The core of the Transformer architecture is the computation of {{c1::scaled dot-product attention}}, which maps queries, keys, and values.
explanation: Scaled dot-product attention measures the similarity between Queries and Keys, scales the results by the square root of key dimension \(\sqrt{d_k}\) to prevent vanishing gradients, applies Softmax, and multiplies by Values.
spanish: El núcleo de la arquitectura Transformer es el cálculo de la atención de producto escalar escalado (scaled dot-product attention), que mapea consultas, claves y valores.

text: A standard encoder-decoder {{c1::Transformer}} block consists of multi-head self-attention, layer normalization, residual connections, and position-wise feed-forward networks.
explanation: Transformers discard recurrence (RNNs) entirely, using self-attention to process tokens in parallel, which makes them highly scalable for training on GPUs.
usage: First introduced in the seminal paper 'Attention Is All You Need' by Vaswani et al. in 2017.
spanish: Un bloque Transformer estándar codificador-decodificador consta de autoatención de múltiples cabezales, normalización de capa, conexiones residuales y redes de alimentación hacia adelante según la posición.

text: To split natural language text into sub-word units before feeding them to an LLM, current models utilize {{c1::Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE)}}.
explanation: Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE) is a sub-word tokenization algorithm. It begins with a character-level vocabulary and iteratively merges the most frequent byte pairs in a corpus to create a fixed-size vocabulary.
usage: Tokenizers like tiktoken (OpenAI) or SentencePiece (Meta) implement variants of Byte-Pair Encoding.
spanish: Para dividir el texto en lenguaje natural en unidades de subpalabras antes de ingresarlas a un LLM, los modelos actuales utilizan la codificación de pares de bytes (BPE).

text: To learn grammar, world knowledge, and reasoning, base LLMs undergo unsupervised pre-training on large corpora using the {{c1::causal language modeling}} objective.
explanation: Causal language modeling (or next-token prediction) masks future tokens, forcing the model to predict the next token given only the left context: \(P(w_t \mid w_1, w_2, \dots, w_{t-1})\).
usage: Decoder-only models (like GPT-4, Llama) are pre-trained using causal language modeling.
spanish: Para aprender gramática, conocimiento del mundo y razonamiento, los LLM base se someten a un preentrenamiento no supervisado en grandes corpus utilizando el objetivo de modelado de lenguaje causal (causal language modeling).

text: To align pre-trained base models so they follow prompts and behave as assistants, engineers perform {{c1::instruction tuning}} (Supervised Fine-Tuning).
explanation: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) trains the base model on high-quality datasets of prompt-response pairs. This adapts the model's output distribution to match user expectations (e.g. conversational format).
usage: SFT datasets consist of structured instructions: {"prompt": "Explain RAG", "response": "..."}.
spanish: Para alinear los modelos base preentrenados de modo que sigan instrucciones y se comporten como asistentes, los ingenieros realizan el ajuste de instrucciones (Supervised Fine-Tuning).

text: In RLHF, human preference data is used to train a {{c1::Reward Model}}, which outputs a scalar score evaluating the quality of an LLM's response.
explanation: The Reward Model is trained using pairwise comparisons (responses A and B, where one is preferred). It acts as the surrogate human evaluator, providing reinforcement signals to optimize the LLM policy.
usage: The Reward Model output is used as the utility function in PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization) loops.
spanish: En RLHF, los datos de preferencia humana se utilizan para entrenar un Modelo de Recompensa (Reward Model), que genera una puntuación escalar que evalúa la calidad de la respuesta de un LLM.

text: To control the randomness of generation, the logits are scaled by dividing them by the {{c1::temperature}} parameter before applying Softmax.
explanation: Scaling logits by temperature \(T\) modifies the probability distribution. High temperature (e.g. \(T=1.2\)) flattens the distribution (diverse output). Low temperature (e.g. \(T=0.1\)) sharpens it (deterministic output).
usage: Logits update: \(p_i = \text{softmax}(z_i / T)\). A temperature of 0 yields greedy decoding.
spanish: Para controlar la aleatoriedad de la generación, los logits se escalan dividiéndolos por el parámetro de temperatura (temperature) antes de aplicar Softmax.

text: The maximum sequence length (in tokens) that an LLM can process in a single execution is called its {{c1::context window}}.
explanation: The context window is determined during training (often bound by self-attention memory complexity). If inputs exceed this limit, the model cannot process them without losing early tokens or suffering quality degradation.
usage: Recent models support context windows from 32K up to 1M+ tokens (e.g. Gemini).
spanish: La longitud máxima de secuencia (en tokens) que un LLM puede procesar en una sola ejecución se denomina ventana de contexto (context window).
